intro: The clearest split between FormatJS and DeepL is how they charge: FormatJS uses free and open source, while DeepL uses a free tier plus paid plans. FormatJS specializes in Internationalization; it fits best for Mobile. DeepL specializes in Translation API; it fits best for Mobile. Pick FormatJS for Mobile; pick DeepL for Mobile. FormatJS is not for hobby projects. DeepL is not for hobby projects. Honest trade-offs: FormatJS is weak for manual deployment is sufficient, and DeepL is weak for manual deployment is sufficient — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the Localization / Translation API umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate. On pricing, FormatJS uses free and open source and DeepL uses a free tier plus paid plans, which materially changes total cost at scale. Lock-in profile also differs: FormatJS is rated low and DeepL is rated low, so exit cost is part of the decision.
quick_take: FormatJS is for mobile; DeepL is for mobile; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose FormatJS if you want free and open source and your workload matches mobile.
verdict_b: Choose DeepL if you want a free tier plus paid plans and your workload matches mobile.

faq.a: FormatJS is the cheaper option on paper since it is open source, while DeepL is paid. FormatJS is free and open source. DeepL offers a free tier.

faq.q: Can I migrate from FormatJS to DeepL?
faq.a: Migration between these is relatively straightforward since both are rated as low lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

FormatJS scores higher on developer experience (5/5 vs 4/5 for DeepL). Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is DeepL a good alternative to FormatJS?
faq.a: DeepL sits in the same category as FormatJS, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. DeepL is best fit for mobile. Note: DeepL is not for hobby projects.
